title: LM_REDUNDANT
text: LM_REDUNDANT The LM_REDUNDANT setting is used when you have a pool of redundant RMS servers and Uniface experiences delays of several seconds when it acquires its licenses from this pool. Generally, Uniface leaves dealing with redundant RMS servers up to Sentinel, which is the best choice. But in some situations, for example, if you logged into an unavailable feature and another feature needs to be tried, or if one of the RMS servers is down, then acquiring licenses may become extremely slow. In these situations, you can add LM_REDUNDANT to your $license_options setting. This allows Uniface to take over the first part of dealing with the redundant server pool. Uniface then starts a separate thread for each server in the list of servers specified in LM_LICENSE_FILE and finds out which server is the main (leader) server. The thread that determines the server it is querying is the leader server will proceed further to check out the first license, and if successful, all subsequent features are tried only on that server. As Sentinel in this case deals with a single server, this setup can be much faster. An example for a pool of three redundant RMS servers: $license_options LM_LICENSE_FILE=rms.serverA;rms.serverB;rms.serverC, LM_REDUNDANT Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
